origin.capability_match: Make POST requests to CCIP-read server (Doc 29)

attack_surface.data_description: Specially crafted ABI-encoded data in POST request to CCIP-read server

security_gap.gap_description: A vulnerability in the CCIP-read server could allow a Relayer to gain unauthorized access or perform unintended actions.

actor_risk.description: A malicious Relayer attempts to exploit vulnerabilities in the CCIP-read server.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If the CCIP-read server has elevated permissions or access to sensitive systems, exploitation could be severe.

assumptions: The CCIP-read server has vulnerabilities in its request handling or business logic (e.g., improper input sanitization, insecure deserialization).
A malicious Relayer can craft a specific request (Asset 211) to trigger such a vulnerability.
Exploiting the vulnerability allows the CCIP-read server to perform actions or access data beyond the Relayer's authorized scope.

mitigation.strategy: Secure coding practices for CCIP-read server, regular security audits, input validation, and principle of least privilege for the server.

description: A malicious Relayer sends a specially crafted POST request to the CCIP-read Server, exploiting a vulnerability to cause the server to perform actions or access data with elevated privileges beyond what is intended for the Relayer.

trace_source.matched_capability: Make POST requests to CCIP-read server (Doc 29)
